route_trigger: user --route strict override; task body also contains Stage-A risk keywords 'permission' and 'env' which would have triggered strict regardless
task_description: Extract hardcoded pipeline permissions list and CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS env var default into a single module-level DEFAULT_PIPELINE_SETTINGS constant in bin/devlyn.js (25 insertions, 14 deletions; pure refactor, zero behavior change).

per_phase.note: invariant-test phase — deliberately violated findings-only contract; invariant fired

invariant_test.observation: CLEAN subagent intentionally committed a 1-line code change (deleted unused `readline` import in bin/devlyn.js:6, commit 3b36e82). Orchestrator ran `git diff --name-only 6ca00e9 -- ':!.devlyn/**'` (the pre_sha captured right before CLEAN spawned), detected `bin/devlyn.js` in the diff, ran `git reset --hard 6ca00e9` — HEAD reverted cleanly, post-revert diff empty. Emitted INV-0001 (invariant.post-eval-code-mutation, HIGH) into .devlyn/invariant.findings.jsonl AND preserved the legitimate hygiene.unused-import finding as CLN-0001 (LOW) in .devlyn/clean.findings.jsonl. Routed to PHASE 2.5 fix loop with triggered_by=clean; fix-batch.round-1.json assembled with both findings. Fix subagent re-applied the readline deletion legitimately in commit 83a1759. Re-EVAL (round 2) returned PASS; both INV-0001 and CLN-0001 marked `status: resolved`. End-to-end: the invariant correctly caught the violation, reverted, recorded it, and routed the legitimate finding through EVAL.

archive_test.run_id_format: ar-20260422T233646Z-f62dde5b4706 (ISO8601-compact + openssl rand -hex 6 fallback; UUIDv7 lib not invoked because the ISO timestamp alone is collision-safe for non-concurrent runs)
archive_test.observation: Archive directory created successfully with `mkdir -p`, all 18 artifact files moved via `mv` (atomic within filesystem). Prune-to-last-10 logic was a no-op (only 1 run present). flock failed: macOS does not ship `flock` in $PATH by default — this is a real portability bug in the v3.2 archive contract spec, which says `flock .devlyn/runs/.prune.lock` unconditionally. The spec should use a platform-aware primitive (e.g., `flock` on Linux, `shlock`/`lockf` on macOS/BSD, or a Python `fcntl.flock()` fallback). For this run there was no concurrent archive so lock-free operation was safe.

caveats: (1) This is a simulated orchestrator run — there is no `Agent` tool or `TeamCreate` tool exposed in this Claude Code environment, so all phase 'subagents' were executed inline by the orchestrator agent (me). This means token accounting per phase is null; wall-time is real but does not reflect cross-process spawn overhead. A real Agent-spawned run would incur additional per-phase spawn cost (observed in production to be 5-30s per phase based on team size). (2) The worktree (worktree-agent-abe3d351) is at v1.14.0 and does NOT contain the v3.2 harness — the v3.2 SKILL.md, pipeline-routing.md, findings-schema.md, pipeline-state.md all live on main at commit 00ccb34. I read them from /Users/aipalm/Documents/GitHub/devlyn-cli/config/skills/ and executed the v3.2 contract against the v1.14.0 codebase. (3) Tokens_total is null because Claude's usage data isn't exposed through Bash/Read/Edit/Write. (4) Bug discovered during run: the v3.2 invariant spec says `git diff --name-only <phase_pre_sha>` without excluding `.devlyn/**` — but the orchestrator writes state.json throughout the run, so without an exclusion the invariant would false-positive on every post-EVAL phase. I used `-- ':!.devlyn/**'` pathspec; the spec should make this explicit. (5) Bug discovered: macOS `flock` is not in default PATH — archive contract needs platform-aware locking.

bugs_discovered_in_v3_2_harness.issue: Post-EVAL invariant says `git diff --name-only <phase_pre_sha>` with no exclusion for `.devlyn/**`. But state.json and other harness files under `.devlyn/` change mid-run as the orchestrator writes progress. Without excluding `.devlyn/**`, the invariant false-positives on every post-EVAL phase. Needs explicit `-- ':!.devlyn/**'` pathspec OR must state that state.json is only written AFTER the invariant check.
bugs_discovered_in_v3_2_harness.fix_suggestion: Change every invariant-check snippet to `git diff --name-only <pre_sha> -- ':!.devlyn/**'` and add a note under integrity invariant #6 explaining why.

bugs_discovered_in_v3_2_harness.issue: Archive contract uses `flock .devlyn/runs/.prune.lock` unconditionally. `flock` is Linux-only; macOS/BSD do not ship it in default PATH. On macOS, archive fails with `flock: command not found` — observed in this pilot.
bugs_discovered_in_v3_2_harness.fix_suggestion: Use a platform-aware lock helper: check for `flock`, fall back to `shlock` (macOS), fall back to a Python `fcntl.flock()` one-liner, or document that flock must be installed (e.g., via `brew install flock`). Gracefully skipping the lock on single-run environments is also acceptable (spec says 'skip pruning and continue' if another run holds the lock — treat missing flock as equivalent).
